title: Podman fails with any dockerfile RUN step with crun
body:
Switched to cgroup v2 and built crun (to see if this would work with SELinux enabled in Ubuntu 20.04.1 as runc is missing a kernel patch).

Running as a rootless user fails with:
sd-bus call: Permission denied (systemd)
write file `/proc/thread-self/attr/exec`: Invalid argument (cgroupfs)

Is there another kernel patch required for Selinux?

comments.body: @giuseppe PTAL

looks like the kernel has no support for selinux, so you need to instruct podman to not use any selinux (or build it without selinux support).

Do you have a file `/proc/self/attr/current`?

Could you try adding `--security-opt selinux=unconfined` to the run command?

Pretty sure the kernel has selinux support in Ubuntu 20.04

$ uname -a
Linux chaasm-build-test 5.4.0-65-generic #73-Ubuntu SMP Mon Jan 18 17:25:17 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

$ sestatus
SELinux status:                 enabled
SELinuxfs mount:                /sys/fs/selinux
SELinux root directory:         /etc/selinux
Loaded policy name:             default
Current mode:                   enforcing
Mode from config file:          enforcing
Policy MLS status:              enabled
Policy deny_unknown status:     allowed
Memory protection checking:     requested (insecure)
Max kernel policy version:      31

We even submitted a pull request and integrated container-selinux:
https://github.com/containers/container-selinux/pull/117

There is a /proc/self/attr/current file (0666):
more current
sysadm_u:sysadm_r:sysadm_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023
We run with confined selinux users (in this case sysadm_u).
I'm not seeing any AVC denials at this point....fixed a bunch of policies (22 in all) for Ubuntu 20.04.

Trying the --security-opt selinux=unconfined command for the podman now...takes a little bit to start the kubernetes debian base image build.

You'll see here that with buildah, adding a debian buster-slim image to the local cache works, and even applies an selinux label to it (doesn't run any RUN commands in the dockerfile):

There is a huge difference between the refpolicy and fedora policies. Currently, I've been able to get cgroup v2 working with cgroupfs on both runc and crun with container-selinux. This was achieved by rewriting the virt policy in refpolicy to the fedora virt policy, and adding the missing types between RH and Ubuntu. The challenge has been systemd-cgroupfs. With the systemd/dbus stream/socket changes, my spidey senses are telling me that the Ubuntu 20.04 LTS included refpolicy might be too old (20190201-8). 

I've built from source the latest 20210203 policy, and plan to pick this back up on Monday by using the latest refpolicy and cross-referencing the fedora policy again. I'll keep the issue posted on the progress.
